behaviors.description: Data from all three sources included in summary input
behaviors.justification: The failure to catch is: one data source gets dropped from the collected data dict during the collection→summary handoff. A unit test of just the collection step might pass even if the dict assembly is broken. Need to test data flow across the collector and verify the assembled dict contains all three sources before summary invocation.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test that: (1) mocks all three data sources with fixed responses, (2) calls the data collection + assembly logic, (3) verifies the returned dict has all three keys with the expected values from each source. This catches accidental omissions where a developer refactors and forgets to include one source in the final dict.

behaviors.description: When one data source unavailable, report produced with 'data unavailable' note instead of aborting
behaviors.justification: The failure to catch is: the script crashes or silently drops the unavailable source instead of gracefully degrading. Could mock one source to raise an exception and verify the others are still queried and the report includes an 'unavailable' note for that section. Pure mocking can catch this; real network errors are covered separately.
behaviors.test_description: Create a parametrized test where each variant: (1) mocks one of the three sources to raise a connection timeout/500 error, (2) calls the data collection logic, (3) verifies the returned dict has data from the other two sources, (4) verifies it contains a flag/note indicating the first source was unavailable. Repeat for each source.

behaviors.description: Narrative summary accurately represents key values in input data
behaviors.justification: The failure to catch is: the LLM-generated summary ignores a field, hallucinates a value, or misinterprets the input data. Mocking the LLM with fixed responses would always pass, hiding real failures where the actual model produces inaccurate or incomplete summaries. Need a real LLM call to verify the narrative truly reflects the input.
behaviors.test_description: Create a test with fixed input data containing specific key values (e.g., 'max latency: 523ms, deployed: 5 services, open issues: 12'). Call the real summary-generation function with a real LLM. Parse the output and verify it includes/reflects those key values in correct form (not hallucinated, not transposed). Run this for multiple input scenarios to catch patterns where the model systematically mishandles certain fields.

behaviors.description: Complete report reaches configured recipient's inbox correctly
behaviors.justification: The failure to catch is: any breakage in the full pipeline from data collection through email delivery. This requires: real data source calls (or test doubles with integration contracts), real LLM summary generation (behavior 3's Agentic dependency), and real SMTP delivery to a real inbox or test inbox with inspection. Mocking any component would hide real failures (data format mismatches, email malformed, lost in pipeline).
behaviors.test_description: End-to-end test that: (1) calls the actual data collection endpoints (or realistic test doubles), (2) invokes the real summary-generation LLM, (3) constructs the full report email, (4) delivers via real SMTP to a test inbox (or sandbox), (5) inspects the inbox to confirm the email arrived with correct subject, sender, and readable body. This catches data loss, encoding errors, email rejections, and LLM-output formatting issues that break email parsing.

behaviors.description: When email unreachable, report saved locally and retried on next scheduled run
behaviors.justification: The failure to catch is: the script fails to save the file, fails to schedule the retry, or the retry doesn't actually execute on the next scheduled run. To verify the retry is *executed* (not just queued), need to test the interaction between the application logic and the real scheduler. Mocking the scheduler would hide failures where the cron job or systemd timer doesn't actually trigger.
behaviors.test_description: Test that: (1) mocks the email service to raise an unreachable error, (2) runs the report generation + delivery, (3) verifies the report file is saved to the configured directory, (4) verifies a retry entry is logged/queued, (5) simulates or waits for the next scheduled run to trigger (either by advancing real time or mocking the scheduler state), (6) verifies the retry is actually executed and email is sent (or fails gracefully again). This requires real filesystem I/O and scheduler integration.
